title: PINNACLE TOWER MANAGEMENT**
content:
**PINNACLE TOWER MANAGEMENT**
**INTERNAL MEMORANDUM**

---

**TO:** All Mailroom Staff  
**FROM:** Patricia Blackwell, Facilities Supervisor  
**DATE:** 14 November 2024  
**RE:** Updated Parcel Sorting Procedures & Holiday Schedule Adjustments  
**FLOOR:** Ground Level – Mailroom Operations

---

This memo serves to clarify recent changes to our daily sorting operations and to inform staff of upcoming schedule modifications.

**PARCEL HANDLING UPDATES:**

Effective immediately, all incoming packages must be logged in the designated ledger with sender name, recipient department, and timestamp. We've noticed inconsistencies in recent weeks—some items have been processed without proper documentation. This is unacceptable and may result in delivery delays to tenants.

Additionally, please ensure that packages weighing over 15kg are placed on the lower shelves to prevent strain injuries. Our insurance provider has requested this safety measure.

**HOLIDAY COVER:**

Tom Hartley will be taking leave from 22-29 November. During this period, temporary cover will be provided by the agency. Please brief them on our standard procedures, including the sign-in protocol for external couriers.

**LOCKER MAINTENANCE:**

Maintenance will be conducting locker inspections on 16 November. All personal items should be removed beforehand. Lockers 7A through 7D require lock replacement due to wear.

**MISCELLANEOUS:**

The break room microwave has been repaired. Please continue to label your lunch items clearly.

Best regards,

Patricia Blackwell  
Facilities Supervisor  
Pinnacle Tower Management

FROM: Derek Chen, Mailroom Supervisor
DATE: 14 November 2024
RE: Updated Sorting Procedures & Holiday Cover Schedule

---

Please be advised of the following operational updates effective immediately:

**1. PACKAGE LABELING STANDARDS**

All incoming parcels must now display legible floor destination labels. We've had continued issues with illegible handwriting causing delays. Use the label printer by the intake desk — do not write directly on boxes. If destination is unclear, contact the sender before processing.

**2. HOLIDAY STAFFING**

The Christmas/New Year period will require adjusted hours. Volunteers needed for:
- 23 Dec – 2 Jan extended shifts
- Night sorting (6pm–2am) 
- Weekend coverage

Interested staff should sign the roster posted above the break room fridge by 20 November. Overtime rates apply.

**3. LOCKER MAINTENANCE**

Facilities will be conducting quarterly locker inspections week of 18 November. Please ensure your assigned locker is clean and contains only personal items. Unclaimed items will be donated. Check the notice board for your inspection time slot.

**4. COURIER ACCOUNT UPDATES**

Our preferred carriers have updated their tracking systems. DX, UPS, and FedEx shipments now require manual code entry into the new database. Training session scheduled for Thursday 16 November at 10am in Conference Room B1. Attendance mandatory for all staff handling external deliveries.

**5. VISITOR LOG PROCEDURES**

Reminder: All visitors entering the building must sign in at reception. Do not process deliveries from couriers who bypass standard check-in procedures.

---

Derek Chen
Mailroom Supervisor, Floor 1
ext. 2847

**Floor 1 | Reference: NM-FL1-0847**

---

**DATE:** 14 November 2024  
**FROM:** Facilities Management  
**TO:** All Mailroom Staff  
**RE:** Noise Complaints & Operational Adjustments

---

**ISSUE SUMMARY:**

We have received three complaints this week regarding noise levels in the mailroom between 08:30-09:15 hours. The primary source has been identified as the new sorting carousel (installed October 2024, Unit Serial: PPS-2847).

**DETAILS:**

- The carousel motor produces an intermittent grinding sound when processing large batch shipments
- Complaints logged by: Reception (Floor G), Finance Department staff passing through
- The sound peaks when the system processes parcels larger than 40cm in length
- Maintenance was last performed 6 weeks ago

**ACTIONS TAKEN:**

1. ~~Temporary relocation of sorting carousel~~ (Not feasible - impacts mail flow)
2. Noise dampening panels ordered - ETA 21 November
3. Carousel operation shifted to 09:30-16:00 window to minimize impact on morning arrivals
4. Maintenance team to conduct full inspection of bearing assembly

**TEMPORARY WORKAROUND:**

Staff are advised to schedule heavy sorting operations after 10:00 when foot traffic in adjacent corridors is lighter. Please notify the mailroom supervisor (ext. 1204) if noise levels exceed acceptable thresholds.

**EQUIPMENT NOTE:**

The older carousel (Unit PPS-1821) remains operational as backup if required.

---

**Follow-up inspection scheduled:** 21 November 2024

### AMBIENT NOISE ASSESSMENT

**Monday 15th November**
- 08:14 - Sorting machine (Belt A) producing intermittent squealing. Maintenance flag raised. Likely worn bearing.
- 09:47 - Delivery vehicle reversing into loading bay. Standard beeping pattern.
- 11:23 - Staff break room adjacent area: elevated conversation levels (typical for 11:15-11:45 period). Appears to be birthday acknowledgment.
- 14:06 - Parcel scanner malfunction alert - repetitive error tone for 3 minutes. IT support called.

**Tuesday 16th November**
- 07:52 - Early shift arrival. Locker doors opening/closing cycle (repeated 12 times over 8 minutes).
- 10:31 - Water cooler refill delivery. Dolly wheels on tile flooring creates expected noise spike.
- 13:44 - Unidentified metallic clattering from northwest corner. Traced to fallen packing tape dispenser. No damage.
- 16:18 - HVAC ventilation duct cleaning equipment active in corridor outside mailroom. Scheduled maintenance confirmed.

**Wednesday 17th November**
- 06:58 - Night shift supervisor keycard entry. Standard access tone.
- 09:12 - Printer jam in administrative office (adjacent). Multiple alert beeps.
- 12:03 - Multiple consecutive delivery arrivals. Highest daily volume noted. Standard operational noise.
- 15:47 - Fluorescent light fitting (Section C) producing intermittent buzzing. Ballast replacement scheduled for week of 22nd.

**Thursday 18th November**
- 08:26 - Regular noise baseline observed.
- 11:09 - Courier collection vehicle horn (external). Building standard.
- 14:22 - Staff moving cardboard recycling bins. Normal operational activity.
- 17:03 - End-of-shift equipment shutdown sequence.

**Friday 19th November**
- 09:15 - Escalated conversation in mailroom proper. Appears to be staff discussion regarding weekend staffing rota. Elevated but not concerning volume levels.
- 10:44 - Package shredder operational. Standard industrial noise profile.
- 13:30 - Occasional banging from loading bay roller shutter maintenance work (external contractor). Approved work order on file.

### OBSERVATIONS

All recorded sound events fall within expected operational parameters for a commercial mailroom facility. No structural integrity concerns detected. HVAC system performing nominally. Recommend replacement of fluorescent ballast (Section C) to eliminate low-level harmonic noise.

**Recommendation:** Routine monitoring to continue. No immediate action required.

**SUBJECT: RE: Floor 1 Mailroom - Quarterly Supply Requisition**

FROM: margaret.chen@pinnaclemanagement.com
TO: tom.hartley@pinnaclemanagement.com
CC: facilities.london@pinnaclemanagement.com
DATE: 14 March 2024

---

Tom,

Following up on your email from last week regarding the Q2 supply order. I've reviewed the requisition forms you submitted and had a few clarifications:

1. **Packaging Materials**: The increase from 400 to 650 boxes of B4 envelopes seems high. Can you confirm if this is due to increased mail volume or a correction from last quarter's miscalculation? Our usage projections from Finance don't quite align.

2. **Printer Ink**: Why are we ordering so much cyan and magenta? The black ink request is standard, but the colour cartridges are unusual for mailroom operations. Have you checked with reception about their printer needs?

3. **Storage**: I noted you requested additional shelving units. Are the current units in locker rows 7 and 8 reaching capacity? If so, we may need to look at archiving older delivery logs to the basement storage facility.

Please get back to me by end of week so I can submit this to Pinnacle Management for approval. We're already three weeks into March and I'd like to avoid delays with our supplier.

Also, quick note — I noticed the sign-in book on your desk looked a bit worn. Let me know if you need a replacement. Standard protocol is to update it monthly, so we should probably refresh it for April anyway.

Thanks,
Margaret

---

**SUBJECT: RE: RE: Floor 1 Mailroom - Quarterly Supply Requisition**

FROM: tom.hartley@pinnaclemanagement.com
TO: margaret.chen@pinnaclemanagement.com
DATE: 15 March 2024

---

Margaret,

Cheers for flagging those. Quick answers:

1. **Boxes**: You're right to question. We had a system error last quarter that duplicated some incoming shipments in the log — hence why we're ordering more stock to make up for the discrepancy.

2. **Ink**: That's on reception. They mentioned their colour printer was acting up and asked if we could order supplies here and bill them. I can ask them to submit their own requisition instead if that's easier for accounting.

3. **Storage**: Locker row 7 is getting tight, yes. I'll start moving archived logs down.

The sign-in book is fine for now, but I'll let you know if it needs refreshing.

-Tom

title: INTERNAL MEMO - MAILROOM OPERATIONS**

**INTERNAL MEMO - MAILROOM OPERATIONS**

**TO:** Floor 1 Staff
**FROM:** Patricia Chen, Mailroom Supervisor
**DATE:** March 14, 2024
**RE:** Updated Delivery Procedures & Lost & Found Notices

---

**DELIVERY LOG REMINDERS**

All personnel are reminded that signature requirements have been updated per corporate policy. Please ensure packages over 2kg require recipient confirmation. Tom Hartley's shift schedule has been adjusted—he will now cover morning routes (6am-2pm) Monday through Friday.

**LOST & FOUND ANNOUNCEMENTS**

The following items remain unclaimed in our office:

- Black umbrella (week of Feb 26)
- Pair of reading glasses in brown case (found near sorting station)
- USB charging cable (no identifying marks)
- Thermal lunch container, green with white lid

Items will be donated after 30 days. Please check with reception if you've lost anything.

**PACKAGE FORWARDING ADDRESSES**

Effective immediately, all international mail must be logged in the new system. Parcels destined for satellite offices should use the standard routing form available from the front desk.

**MAINTENANCE NOTICE**

Locker bank C (lockers 1-50) will be serviced March 18-19. Please remove any items. Lockers 51-100 remain available.

**UPCOMING HOLIDAY SCHEDULE**

The mailroom will operate on reduced hours during the Easter holiday period (April 8-12). Non-urgent mail will be held until April 15. For time-sensitive deliveries, contact the office manager.

---

Questions? Email: mailroom@pinnacletower.co.uk
